name: Kevin F.
education: B.S., Computer Engineering from University of Washington; North Kitsap High School
headline: Principal Software Engineering Manager at Microsoft
about:
I am passionate about how technology can be used to help improve people's lives.  Whether it is though my work, my teaching, or my leadership I look to define the role technology can take to solve problems in unique and revolutionary ways.  

Right now I am focused on cloud networking   at Microsoft. I work with an amazing team to develop software solutions to enable the largest customers of Azure to manage their networks at scale.  Prior to my current role I am focused on cybersecurity at Microsoft and loved the challenges of helping protect one of the largest companies in the world from cyber attacks.  I continue to have a passion in understanding network security through reducing attack surface area to minimize 

I elegantly bridge the gap between technology, business, and people to apply the best technology solutions to the problems at hand. I can translate complex technological ideas into simple language for people without a technical background, translating their vision and needs into the best technology solutions.  

I believe in developing strong team collaboration that pulls people together by focusing on shared goals to achieve the best possible outcomes.  Learning is critical to the success of any venture and I firmly believe in providing and accepting feedback that helps myself and others continue to grow. 

As an Information Technology expert I possess both a breadth and depth of technical knowledge across a wide range of areas including Software Development, Software Development Lifecycle, Networking, Information Security, Storage, Databases, Virtualization, n-Tier Applications Development and Deployment, Server and Service platforms, and Client technologies.

I am a Software Engineer and Architect. I have served as the principal software engineer and lead architect on many enterprise, mission-critical applications utilizing a variety of development methodologies, best practices, design patterns, Agile and other SDLCs, test driven development (TDD), and requirements gathering.

experiences.title: Guest Lecturer
experiences.jobDescription: After taking 5 years off, I am back to teaching part time at the University of Washington! I focus on teaching about database and data systems design.

experiences.title: Principal Software Engineer Manager
experiences.jobDescription: Building enterprise level networking products in Azure

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineering Manager
experiences.jobDescription: Working on Azure Virtual Network Manager

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Working on Azure Software Defined Networking.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineering Manager
experiences.jobDescription: I am currently focused on evaluating the Network Isolation space across the Microsoft server fleet to ensure the network surface area is minimized with a focus on protecting Microsoft from network intruders.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Worked on building a software platform to manage Microsoft network surface area in a consistent, compliant way to minimize open networks across the server fleet.

experiences.title: Guest Lecturer
experiences.jobDescription: Instructed core undergraduate course in databases focusing on fundamentals of database design, query, and operation. Covered relational databases as well as NoSQL, with a focus on relevant, current technologies.

experiences.title: Chief Technology Officer
experiences.jobDescription: Manage all aspects of software development and technology application for the organization, including running the Seattle development office.  I meet with leaders of medical labs across the company to work to develop technology solutions to meet some of the most complex laboratory problems.

experiences.companyName: University of Washington - School of Medicine
experiences.title: IT & Software Engineering Manager
experiences.jobDescription:
Manager of software development and new technologies in the Department of Pathology.  My journey at the UW took me through all aspects of technology.  I lead the helpdesk team for a while, managed all the servers and took on a site engineering role, and later become mostly focused on leading software development efforts.  

Some direct software projects lead to massive cost savings and efficiencies in the University around procurement and employee time management.  Medically I developed first in the nation software that lead to great increases in patient safety and efficiencies in managing patient care.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Embedded software engineer.  Developed systems to work with cellular communication towers.

experiences.title: Helpdesk Technician
experiences.jobDescription: Provided primarily helpdesk services for a local ISP.  Also developed web based software including an eCommerce solution.

publications.title: Bar Code-Driven Cassette Printing Dramatically Improves Quality and Efficiency
publications.source: Colllege of American Pathologists - Advancing Practice, Instruction, and Innovation Through Informatics (APIII) 2009 Abstracts
publications.summary: Bar code–driven workflow has the potential for improving the efficiency, patient safety, and economics of anatomic pathology laboratory operations, yet there are few quantitative data to support its use. Potential benefits are both direct (error reduction, personnel savings, reduced wastage) and indirect (reduced work associated with error correction and decreased medical-legal liability). Efficiency analyses suggest that the most reliable and efficient systems are those that incorporate single-piece, continuous workflow with just-in-time printing, and that avoid any manual labeling steps. This report details benefits and costs associated with implementing cassette bar coding.

publications.title: Quality and Efficiency in Pathology-Photography
publications.source: Colllege of American Pathologists - Advancing Practice, Instruction, and Innovation Through Informatics (APIII) 2007 Abstracts
publications.summary: photography offers the potential for dramatic improvement in the efficiency of pathology imaging, not just through storage of images but also through re-engineered workflow processes to capture images. We analyzed the workflow involved in capturing and storing gross photos, photomicrographs, electron micrographs, and immunofluorescence images and then developed middleware to facilitate their acquisition and storage in our laboratory information system (LIS). For each image type, we quantified the efficiency and quality benefits.

recommendationsReceived.name: David Leveille
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Microsoft 365 & Power Platform Architect | Cloud Solutions, Microsoft 365, Copilot & Power Platform | Enabling Digital Transformation
recommendationsReceived.context: David was senior to Kevin but didn't manage Kevin directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Kevin is first of all a great person. He is an extremely reliable, competent, and highly motivated technology expert. Additionally, he constantly improves his skills. I really enjoyed working with Kevin at Silverlink and the University of Washington!

recommendationsReceived.name: Kyle Rowland
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Mission Enabler & Money Saver
recommendationsReceived.context: Kyle worked with Kevin but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Kevin is a top shelf software engineer - due mostly to a deep passion for technology and computing he is a constant source of technical inspiration and visionary insight.  Collaborating with him on ANYTHING is a pleasure.  You should only choose this guy for your team if you want to take your group to the next level while developing the highest quality product.

recommendationsReceived.name: Samuel Skjonsberg
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Engineering Manager at Runway
recommendationsReceived.description: Kevin served as a fantastic manager.   He provided excellent guidance and an open environment for growth.   He was also an extremely important source of technical knowledge/expertise.  I'd definitely recommend Kevin as a great technical lead, manager, and software developer.

recommendationsReceived.name: Lucas Reber
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Technology Leader focused on building collaborative communities, cloud computing, and providing secure access to technology
recommendationsReceived.context: Lucas worked with Kevin but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Kevin is an excellent programmer and systems administrator. It has been a pleasure working with him on numerous projects around the university.

recommendationsReceived.name: Mike Middlebrooks
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: IT Director at University of Washington
recommendationsReceived.context: Mike worked with Kevin on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Kevin is a incredibly creative developer, a sparkler of ideas.  He is a solid gold performer on our staff.

recommendationsReceived.name: Barb Prentiss
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Retired Director of Information Technology, School of Medicine,University of Washington
recommendationsReceived.context: Barb was senior to Kevin but didn't manage Kevin directly
recommendationsReceived.description: I've worked with Kevin in several roles over the past 10 years that he's worked in our department, from direct supervisor to project member on a develpment projects he has led.  Kevin's depth of knowledge linked with his ability to understand user needs and his committment to excellence are some of his greatest assets; all of which make him  invaluable to our organization.  He has developed and implemented several in-house custom programs to stream-line business operations and assure that we meet federal and state compliance mandates. Some of these products are being used throughout the School of Medicine and are being analyzed for campus implementation.  He is a campus resource for other IT/IS professionals as well as other departments for his expertise.  In addition to being highly productive, his sense of humor and easy demeanor make working with Kevin very enjoyable.

recommendations.name: Alexia Chan
recommendations.subtitle: Healthcare Coordinator @ PDS Health | UW Informatics Alum
recommendations.description: Alexia was great to work with! She was able to handle the workload and come up with labs and other support for students as needed. She is an independent worker but also knew when to ask for help when needed. She is a pleasant person to work with and I enjoyed having her as my TA.

recommendations.name: Aaron Sarnat
recommendations.subtitle: On sabbatical
recommendations.description:
Aaron is one of those unique individuals that you don't encounter every day and his ability to bring professionalism and talent is unsurpassed. We were a small software development company creating software and were in need of a User Experience (UX) / User Interface (UI) expert and Aaron was exactly what we were looking for.  

We didn't have much experience with UX /UI design and Aaron was incredibly helpful walking us through the process and defining a schedule and an outline of what was required.  He met with us, took the time to understand our needs and the business that we operate in, and was able to create a great synergy with me and my team.

He is very detail oriented in his time estimates, budget conscious and up-front with determining costs.  I never experienced any unexpected costs when I got his monthly bill and felt very satisfied with the work delivered.

Aaron worked collaboratively with us and was always available for communication.  He didn't disappear after our initial meeting and come back with a finished product.  Instead he worked together with us to suggest different design ideas and together we iteratively refined the design until we both were satisfied. 

Aaron is not just a graphic designer, and while he is definitely able to make great designs, his real ability is to understand how users will interact with the software and work to develop the interactions.  We found this to be incredibly useful as he wasn't just about the colors and layout, but also how to users would actually user the software.

I plan to engage Aaron in the future for any other UI/UX projects we have and fully would recommend to anyone.

recommendations.name: Anton Grobman
recommendations.subtitle: Software Developer
recommendations.description: Anton worked with me as a software developer on a variety of projects.  He was very project focused and worked independently, a real plus in our organization.  He had the initiative and drive to work with the various stakeholders of the applications he was developing and took the lead to develop a project plan, software requirements, and ultimately develop and deliver the software.  It was a pleasure to work with Anton.

recommendations.name: Anton Grobman
recommendations.subtitle: Software Developer
recommendations.description: Anton was a pleasure to have in the classroom.  He provided upbeat, intelligent conversation during group work and always brought good perspectives and ideas to the classroom.

recommendations.name: Calen Kaneko, P.E.
recommendations.subtitle: Mechanical Design Engineer at TerraPower
recommendations.description: I employed Calen while he completed his undergraduate degree at the University of Washington.  Calen is a hard worker and was able to accomplish any task I sent his way.  He was technically savvy and able to solve many challenges in our diverse environment.  Calen was a front line tech within the computer support division in the department.  Calen was able to work well with people and provided great customer service.

recommendations.name: Samuel Skjonsberg
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Engineering Manager at Runway
recommendations.context: Kevin was senior to Samuel but didn't manage Samuel directly
recommendations.description: Sam brought a professional and intelligent attitude to his job as with us in Pathology Computer Support.  He worked very well in teams and possessed a strong work ethic.  When I assigned Sam a task, I knew it would be completed on time and with minimally "hand-holding" from me.  Sam took on new technologies and projects, finding resources to learn about whatever project he had to accomplish.  I would recommend Sam for any technical position, whether it be helpdesk, server management, or software development.

Principal Software Eng Manager at Microsoft

summary: Kevin F. is currently a Principal Software Engineer Manager at Microsoft and serves as a Guest Lecturer at the University of Washington Information School, combining enterprise-scale product leadership with academic teaching. His LinkedIn posts consistently focus on Azure cloud networking—announcing features, previews, and GA releases (Azure Virtual Network Manager, Routing Appliance, NSP, IPAM, VNV, UDR management, mesh and hub/spoke connectivity) and often include concrete technical details (limits, throughput, billing model changes). His tone mixes technical depth and product leadership with personal reflection and team recognition: he explains engineering tradeoffs, highlights performance/security improvements, thanks collaborators, and occasionally shares career anecdotes from his earlier engineering work. Recurring themes are scale, operational simplicity, security-first design, customer pain-point solving, and alignment of billing to managed resources; he demonstrates familiarity with both architecture and implementation concerns. Posts show a pattern of practical problem-solving (how to manage routing, IP allocations, private endpoints at scale), community engagement (links to docs, labs, shout-outs to colleagues and external experts), and recruiting/hiring calls for engineers. Overall his content differentiates itself by blending hands-on technical expertise in cloud networking with product thinking, customer empathy, and an educator’s desire to enable others to learn and adopt the technologies.
highlights: Product Leader — Feature launches & operational design
